generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ChassisController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Supervisory controller that automatically adjusts chassis parameters for stability and performance optimization based on DT predictions and PT feedback"
]

instance ForceOptimizer : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithm that determines optimal force distribution configurations for dynamic performance improvement"
]

instance ActuatorRegulator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Regulatory system implementing PID controllers for real-time actuator adjustments to maintain system safety and operational conditions"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring two internal temperature points with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring one external temperature point with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance EnvironmentalForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor array detecting external forces acting on the PT system (e.g., aerodynamic forces, road surface dynamics)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ForceParameterStream
]

instance OperationalStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive sensor suite tracking system metrics (speed, acceleration, wheel load distribution) for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalMetricsStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor, ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data stream from internal/external sensors for model calibration and validation"
]

instance ForceParameterStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalForceSensor
    base:desc "Real-time environmental force parameter stream facilitating dynamic model calibration"
]

instance OperationalMetricsStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalStateSensor
    base:desc "Continuous operational metrics stream including speed, acceleration, and load distribution for performance monitoring"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DifferentialEquationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Abstract representation of dynamic system behavior using differential equations to model force dynamics, motion trajectories, and environmental interactions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

instance GeometricCADModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static CAD representation capturing physical dimensions (wheel sizes, chassis shapes) for geometric modeling"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

instance HybridModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Combination of geometric and behavioral models enabling optimization tasks like fault detection and state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance CalibrationParameterDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Centralized database storing calibrated model parameters derived from sensor data for continuous system representation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream, ForceParameterStream, OperationalMetricsStream
]

instance SimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Environmental parameters database containing current and forecasted conditions affecting PT performance"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing historical operational data for predictive analytics and validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalMetricsStream
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data synchronization server facilitating near-real-time transmission of structured data types (environmental parameters, system metrics) between PT and DT"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationModel, GeometricCADModel, HybridModel
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics simulation engine handling dynamic behavior modeling (force-based simulations for PT movement prediction)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationModel
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dynamic calibration system updating models with real-time parameter estimates from sensor data to minimize estimation errors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HybridModel
]

instance StateEstimationFilter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithm processing noisy sensor data to provide accurate PT state representations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OperationalMetricsStream
]

instance OptimizationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework implementing optimization algorithms for finding optimal system configurations (e.g., energy efficiency, performance metrics)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ForceOptimizer
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring service tracking system performance, safety, and operational health through continuous sensor data analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimePerformanceInsight, SafetyViolationInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance optimization service determining optimal system configurations through model-based predictions"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
    DTDFVocab:provides EfficiencyOptimizationAction, EnergyConsumptionAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics service simulating PT behavior under hypothetical conditions for proactive decision-making"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService
    DTDFVocab:provides FutureStatePrediction, FaultScenarioPrediction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated control service implementing supervisory controllers to adjust PT behavior when safety/performance violations detected"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorAdjustmentAction, ParameterTuningAction
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model calibration service dynamically updating system representations with real-time sensor data for improved accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction, ModelRefinementAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization service presenting complex DT outputs in accessible formats for engineers and stakeholders"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceDashboard, ModelParameterVisualization
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimePerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous performance metrics analysis showing current system behavior under operating conditions (speed, acceleration, load distribution)"
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts triggered when system parameters exceed predefined safety thresholds (e.g., excessive acceleration, structural stress)"
]

instance EfficiencyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action recommending optimal parameter configurations to improve energy efficiency and operational performance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyConsumptionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action suggesting adjustments to reduce energy consumption while maintaining performance requirements"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FutureStatePrediction : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted system behavior under forecasted conditions (e.g., force dynamics, trajectory deviations) based on current model state"
]

instance FaultScenarioPrediction : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated fault conditions analysis identifying potential failure modes and their impacts on system performance"
]

instance ActuatorAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of physical actuators to correct performance deviations detected by control algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterTuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Algorithm-driven parameter adjustments to optimize system performance based on real-time monitoring data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic model parameter updates to reflect real-time sensor data and improve DT accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Process of continuously refining system models through calibration iterations to maintain alignment with PT behavior"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization interface showing real-time system performance metrics, model predictions, and calibration status"
]

instance ModelParameterVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representation of model parameters, calibration accuracy metrics, and their impact on system behavior"
]

// C12: Environment
instance OperationalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalSystem, ExternalConditions
]

instance PhysicalSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "The Cyber-Physical System being modeled (e.g., automotive chassis, industrial machinery) with embedded sensors and actuators"
    base:isContainedIn OperationalEnvironment
]

instance ExternalConditions : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Environmental factors affecting the PT (e.g., temperature variations, road surface conditions, aerodynamic forces)"
    base:isContainedIn OperationalEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system in the provided context emphasizes real-time synchronization between the Physical Twin (PT) and its digital counterpart through **continuous data collection** from sensors over time. The temporal aspects are governed by the **calibration algorithms** that update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with the best parameter estimates, ensuring alignment with the PT’s operational conditions. While the documents do not specify explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds), the DT relies on **latency-sensitive algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to process data in near-real-time, enabling immediate detection of deviations (violations) and triggering adaptive interventions. The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **operating conditions** of the PT, where model updates and monitoring services must operate at scales sufficient to capture dynamic behaviors without introducing unacceptable delays. The absence of explicit time scales suggests a **flexible, adaptive time-scale approach** where synchronization is dynamically adjusted based on system demands and criticality levels."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the documents is structured as a **closed-loop, centralized architecture** where the DT integrates **three core components**:
  1. **Data Collection**: Continuous sensor data from the PT feeds into the DT to inform model calibration and monitoring.
  2. **Models**: Abstract representations of the PT’s cyber-physical behavior (e.g., differential equations for dynamics) and its environment, updated via calibration algorithms.
  3. **Algorithms**: Techniques such as **fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimisers** process data and models to generate insights and trigger interventions.
  The orchestration relies on **real-time synchronization** between the PT and DT, with **calibration algorithms** dynamically refining models to match PT behavior. The system lacks explicit mention of **orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or **resource management** (e.g., cloud scaling, edge computing), implying a **simplified, monolithic approach** where all components operate within a single, tightly coupled framework. Coordination is implicit through the **feedback loop** between data, models, and algorithms, ensuring alignment with PT conditions."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle encompasses phases centered around **modeling, simulation, monitoring, and optimization** of the Physical Twin (PT), with distinct stages and representation types:
    **1. Design Phase**:
       - Abstract representation of the system (e.g., CAD models) focuses on static attributes (e.g., wheel sizes, chassis shapes) but lacks dynamic behavior (e.g., force dynamics).
       - Dynamic models (e.g., differential equations) are later integrated to analyze system behavior under operational conditions.
    **2. Development Phase**:
       - DT integrates **data** (sensor-collected PT data over time), **models** (cyber-physical knowledge of PT and environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detection, state estimation, optimizers).
       - Calibration algorithms update models with real-time PT data to refine parameter estimates (e.g., for CPS and environmental modeling).
    **3. Deployment Phase**:
       - DT becomes operational, enabling real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and decision-making via closed-loop systems (e.g., supervisory controllers triggering physical adjustments).
    **Lifecycle Governance**:
       - Models are **not discarded** post-deployment but persist as part of the DT framework, ensuring continuity across phases.
       - Transition management relies on iterative validation (e.g., comparing DT predictions with PT performance) to maintain accuracy and relevance.
    **Note**: The provided documents do not specify explicit phase names or governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards), but the lifecycle is implicitly defined by the DT’s **integration of modeling, monitoring, and optimization** stages.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
  The DT prioritizes **high-fidelity models** to replicate the PT’s behavior accurately under current operating conditions. Fidelity is ensured through:
  - **Model Types**: Emphasis on **dynamic models** (e.g., differential equations) for behavior prediction, while geometric models serve as static references.
  - **Validation/Verification**: Models are validated via **real-time calibration** using sensor data, ensuring parameter accuracy. No explicit verification methods (e.g., finite element analysis) are mentioned.

  **Uncertainty Quantification**:
  Uncertainty arises from **model mismatches** (e.g., between static and dynamic representations) and **sensor noise**. The DT mitigates this via:
  - **Error Detection**: Monitors flag deviations between PT and DT behavior, prompting recalibration.
  - **Quality Assurance**: Continuous integration of sensor data into models maintains **operational fidelity**, though no formal error bounds or quantification methods are specified.

  **Error Correction Mechanisms**:
  The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively adjust models based on sensor feedback, reducing discrepancies between PT and DT. No explicit error correction algorithms (e.g., Kalman filters) are detailed, but the framework assumes adaptive recalibration for dynamic systems.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a **RabbitMQ server** for synchronization between components, ensuring reliable data exchange within the system. While the document does not specify communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, TCP/IP), network topology (e.g., star, mesh), or bandwidth/latency requirements, it implies a **high-reliability** infrastructure for real-time or near-real-time communication. No explicit security protocols (e.g., TLS, authentication) or redundancy mechanisms are mentioned, though the use of a centralized synchronization server suggests a potential for redundancy if deployed in distributed environments."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    Not in Document
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system incorporates a **safe protocol** integrated into the low-level driver to ensure hardware/software safety. Safety is validated through **monitoring mechanisms** that assess whether the system operates safely and optimally by comparing real-time performance against predefined criteria. While the DT integrates **calibration algorithms** to update models with sensor data for parameter estimation, there is no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols, authentication systems, fail-safe mechanisms, or risk assessment frameworks beyond the basic operational safety checks tied to the low-level driver implementation."
]
